title: The Iteration Protocol
beginnerExplanation: The Iteration Protocol is a contract: any class implementing __iter__() and __next__() becomes an Iterator and can be used directly in 'for' loops.
intermediateExplanation: A loop secretly works by calling iter() on an object, then calling next() inside a try/except block that catches StopIteration to terminate the loop.
advancedExplanation: Iterators must return self in their __iter__() implementation. Custom iterators must raise StopIteration inside __next__() when there are no more values.

realWorldAnalogy.title: Vending Machine
realWorldAnalogy.description: Inserting a coin activates the machine (iter()). Pressing the button dispenses one item (next()). When empty, the machine shows a SOLD OUT light (StopIteration).

mentalModel: A protocol allows any object to pose as a sequence by answering 'what is next?' until it has no more answers.
visualDiagram: for x in obj -> iter(obj) -> next() -> return x -> next() -> StopIteration -> exit loop
executionWalkthrough: 1. Loop starts, calling __iter__() on the target.
2. The returned iterator is queried repeatedly using __next__().
3. When __next__() raises StopIteration, Python catches the error and cleanly exits the loop.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to implement __iter__ in an Iterator (iterators must be iterable too!).
Infinite loops when custom iterators forget to raise StopIteration.

debuggingMission.scenario: A custom iterator loops forever because the StopIteration condition is never hit.
debuggingMission.solution:
class ThreeTimes:
  def __init__(self): self.c = 0
  def __iter__(self): return self
  def __next__(self):
    self.c += 1
    if self.c > 3: raise StopIteration
    return 3
debuggingMission.explanation: Ensure you increment a counter or check a condition to raise StopIteration.

practiceQuestions.question: What must the __iter__() method of an Iterator class return?
practiceQuestions.explanation: An iterator's __iter__() method must return the iterator itself (self) to allow nested and direct loops.

challengeQuestions.question: What error terminates the default 'for' loop in Python?

projectExercise.title: Custom Countdown Iterator

bossBattle.name: Sentinel of the Stream
bossBattle.description: Build an iterator class that bypasses the Sentinel's defenses.
bossBattle.mechanic: Implement custom iteration steps to count waves.

complexityAnalysis.spaceComplexity: O(1) space since state variables are updated in place.
complexityAnalysis.description: Ideal for constant-space traversal of custom data structures.

memoryImpact.allocationBytes: Small object footprint. Preserves memory by generating sequence on demand.
memoryImpact.optimizationNotes: Always raise StopIteration as soon as the sequence boundary is crossed.

performanceNotes: Defining custom iterators in Python incurs standard method call overhead. C-extensions use faster slots.
